title: True AI Result Accuracy — questions for evaluating an AI-search competitor
html:
True AI Result Accuracy — questions for evaluating an AI-search competitor

Source: 2026-03-13 Apple Note. Yahir's diligence questions for what looks like a competitor or potential acquisition target in the AI visibility engine space — possibly Lovable (named at the bottom).

TL;DR

  • The architecture question: "the shift from API-based to scraping-based architecture — can they actually see what LLM front-end bots see? A lot of audit tools fall short because API results differ from what users actually get." — articulates the scraping-vs-API divide in AEO/GEO tooling.
  • Tiering: "What does the technical implementation to different company needs look like, is it tiered are there phases?" — direct echo of Rankett's tier 1/2/3 framing.
  • Company makeup: "Venture-backed, bootstrapped, how large is the team?" — competitor-fingerprinting.
  • Success metrics: "What specific numbers can they actually put in front of a client to show it's working?"
  • Marketing + closing + fulfillment + systemization: 5 questions in a row about GTM operations — the same questions Diddles is being asked to solve in the Jan 2 call.
  • Single name dropped at end: "Lovable" — likely Lovable.dev or the AI-search audit tool by that name.
  • Plus: "Jeffntc-bird" — username/handle, unidentified.

Pattern observed

Yahir is doing competitor diligence on AI-search-optimization tooling in March 2026 — same period he's doubling down on Rankett partner program (the April 27 doc). The questions reveal what he most cares about in a Rankett rival: scraping-vs-API accuracy, tiering, fulfillment systemization, marketing engine.

This note is operationally adjacent to the April Rankett build doc — same vocabulary (tier 1-3, fulfillment, systemization, monthly retainer).

Open questions

  • Is "Lovable" a competitor he's evaluating, or a tool he's considering integrating?
  • "Jeffntc-bird" — Twitter handle? Internal Diddles team member?
